description: Grading rubric for evaluating skill pack quality across 5 weighted dimensions.

dimensions.name: Trigger Accuracy
dimensions.description: Does the description trigger on specific conditions? Are false positives minimized?

scale.5: Triggers ONLY on specific conditions, zero false positives. Description is precise, keyword-rich, and uses the pushy pattern for implicit triggers.
scale.4: Specific triggers with minimal false positives. Description covers most triggering conditions clearly.
scale.3: Captures most triggering conditions but may miss edge cases or have occasional false positives.
scale.2: Vague description or triggers too broadly. Missing activation context or trigger keywords.
scale.1: Generic or non-triggering description. Agent cannot match to any real task.

checklist: Description starts with 'Use when...' or equivalent activation context
Specific triggering conditions are listed
No generic trigger words that match unrelated tasks
False positives are minimized through boundary conditions
Clear cause-effect relationship stated

dimensions.name: Action Coherence
dimensions.description: Does the skill do ONE thing well? Are entry/exit states clear? Is there mission creep?

scale.5: Single purpose, clear entry/exit states, zero mission creep. Boundaries explicitly marked.
scale.4: Single purpose with minor optional extensions. Entry/exit states mostly clear.
scale.3: Clear primary purpose but some optional scope creep. Boundaries could be tighter.
scale.2: Multiple purposes with unclear boundaries. Skill tries to do too much.
scale.1: Does everything, completely unfocused. No clear purpose statement.

checklist: Single clear purpose stated in the skill body
Entry state defined (when to load the skill)
Exit state defined (when the skill's work is done)
No mission creep in the body content
Boundaries explicitly marked (what the skill does NOT do)

dimensions.name: Reference Integrity
dimensions.description: Is reference depth = 1 level? Are references numbered and ordered? Are there circular references?

scale.5: Perfect 1-level depth, logical numbering, clear TOC for P3. All links resolve.
scale.4: 1-level depth with minor ordering issues. All links resolve.
scale.3: Some references could be consolidated. Minor structural issues.
scale.2: 2-level reference depth or circular references detected.
scale.1: Chaotic reference structure. Broken links, nested references, no ordering.

checklist: Reference depth = 1 level max (no nested references/)
Files numbered for ordering (01-name.md, 02-name.md)
All markdown links resolve to existing files
No circular references between reference files

dimensions.name: Non-Redundancy
dimensions.description: Does this overlap with existing skills? Is the purpose distinct? Can boundaries be clearly drawn?

scale.5: Unique purpose, no overlap with any existing skill. Clear authority boundary.
scale.4: Minimal overlap with clear boundaries. Can explain the difference in one sentence.
scale.3: Some overlap but distinct enough. Boundaries documented.
scale.2: Significant overlap with existing skill. Authority boundary unclear.
scale.1: Duplicate of existing skill. No justification for separate existence.

checklist: No duplicate of existing skill in the same pack or sibling packs
Distinct purpose from similar skills
Boundaries clear and documented
Can explain the difference from adjacent skills in one sentence

dimensions.name: Edge Case Coverage
dimensions.description: Does it handle degraded states? Delegation edge cases? Post-cancel scenarios? Late-session drift?

scale.5: All 6 entry states covered (FRESH, RESUMED, DELEGATED, DEGRADED, POST-CANCEL, LATE). Handles complex edge cases.
scale.4: Most entry states covered. Common edge cases handled.
scale.3: Basic states covered. Some edge case handling present.
scale.2: Only basic states covered. No edge case handling.
scale.1: Does not account for session complexity at all.

checklist: Entry state recognition present
Degraded state handling documented
Delegation edge cases addressed
Post-cancel scenario awareness
Late-session drift handling

GOOD.action: Minor polish only. One targeted fix, then re-score.

ACCEPTABLE.action: Address specific dimension gaps. Iterate up to 2 cycles.

NEEDS_WORK.action: Full refinement loop required. Iterate up to 3 cycles.

scoring.block_rule: Any single dimension scoring ≤2 blocks release regardless of overall score.
